articles.title: Lakers Still Lead Series 3-1 Despite Game‑4 Loss to Rockets
articles.original_title: LeBron James: Lakers Aren’t Comfortable Despite 3-1 Series Lead On Rockets
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers dropped Game 4 to the Houston Rockets, preventing a sweep. LeBron James and the team struggled offensively, but the Lakers retain a 3‑1 series advantage and will host Game 5 to try to close out the series.
key_points: Lakers lost Game 4, ending their chance for a sweep.
LeBron James had a sub‑par performance, reflecting broader team issues.
Series stands at 3‑1 in favor of the Lakers, with Game 5 back in Los Angeles.

articles.title: LeBron James and Luka Dončić Defend Adou Thiero After Game‑4 Ejection
articles.original_title: LeBron James and Luka Dončić stood up for Adou Thiero after Game 4 ejection
articles.summary: Following a controversial ejection of Rockets guard Adou Thiero in the closing minutes of Game 4, both LeBron James and Luka Dončić publicly supported the player, criticizing the officiating. The incident highlighted growing tension over perceived inconsistent calls in the series.
key_points: Thiero was ejected after a minor scuffle with Aaron Holiday, a decision many called questionable.
James and Dončić spoke out, calling the call “ridiculous” and defending Thiero.
The episode added to the series’ mounting frustrations over officiating.

articles.title: Marcus Smart Finds Rockets’ Trash‑Talk ‘Hilarious’ After Game‑4 Drama
articles.original_title: Marcus Smart Thinks Trash-Talk From Jae’Sean Tate & Rockets Was ‘Hilarious’
articles.summary: After the Rockets avoided a sweep by beating the Lakers in Game 4, tensions flared, resulting in multiple technical fouls and ejections. Marcus Smart commented that the Rockets’ trash‑talk, particularly from Jae’Sean Tate, was amusing amid the chaos.
key_points: Game 4 featured several technical fouls and player ejections.
Jae’Sean Tate’s comments sparked a heated exchange with the Lakers.
Smart described the Rockets’ trash‑talk as “hilarious,” underscoring the rivalry’s intensity.

articles.title: Turnover Woes Threaten Lakers’ Sweep of Rockets
articles.original_title: The Lakers have a big turnover problem against the Rockets
articles.summary: In Game 4, the Lakers’ turnover rate surged, allowing the Rockets to stay alive in the series. The lapse, combined with Kevin Durant’s continued absence, gave Houston a chance to extend the series back to Los Angeles for Game 5.
key_points: Lakers committed a high number of turnovers, contributing to the loss.
Kevin Durant remained out, limiting the Lakers’ offensive options.
The Rockets leveraged the mistake to avoid a sweep and force a Game 5.

articles.title: Alperen Şengün Says Deandre Ayton Deserved No Ejection
articles.original_title: Alperen Sengun Doesn’t Think Deandre Ayton Should’ve Been Ejected
articles.summary: Şengün argued that Ayton’s physical defense on him was a key factor in the Lakers taking a 3‑0 series lead over the Rockets. While Ayton’s aggressive play has been effective, a controversial incident in Game 4 led to Ayton’s ejection, which Şengün believes was unwarranted.
key_points: Ayton’s tough defense helped the Lakers dominate early in the series.
A late-game incident in Game 4 resulted in Ayton’s ejection.
Şengün publicly stated the ejection was unnecessary.

key_points: A minor scuffle with about a minute left resulted in two ejections.
Luka Dončić volunteered to pay the fine for the Lakers rookie.

articles.title: Wembanyama Expresses Frustration Over NBA Concussion Protocol

articles.summary: San Antonio Spurs phenom Victor Wembanyama said he is "very unhappy" with how the league handled the concussion protocol after his playoff injury. While he did not disclose specifics, he criticized the return‑to‑play process as inadequate. The comments highlight ongoing concerns about player safety procedures.
key_points: Wembanyama is dissatisfied with the concussion management after his injury.
He did not provide detailed grievances but called the process unsatisfactory.
The remarks raise questions about the NBA’s medical protocols.

articles.title: MRI Confirms Anthony Edwards’ Bone Bruise, Out Week‑to‑Week
articles.original_title: It's official, MRI shows Anthony Edwards has bone bruise in left knee, listed as "week to week"
articles.summary: An MRI has confirmed that Minnesota Timberwolves forward Anthony Edwards suffered a bone bruise in his left knee. The injury sidelines him for the remainder of the first‑round series against the Denver Nuggets and likely beyond. The report lists his status as "week to week" with no immediate return date.
key_points: Edwards diagnosed with a left‑knee bone bruise.
Out for the rest of the Timberwolves’ first‑round series.
Status listed as "week to week," suggesting a longer recovery.

key_points: Celtics and Spurs each need one more win to secure advancement.
